page: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-1xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server configured with 20 shared x86_64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 12.8 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and features a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing 24 GB of dedicated VRAM.

This server configuration does not include local storage, requiring external network storage solutions for persistent data. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU allocation is shared, which may introduce resource contention compared to dedicated CPU instances, representing a key architectural tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained maximum CPU throughput.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio combined with a dedicated accelerator makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive GPU workloads. It is designed for tasks such as machine learning inference, data science pipelines, and graphics rendering, where massive system memory is required alongside GPU acceleration.
